_comment: Tier-2 corpus for the document-store STORAGE PRIMITIVE (writeDatabaseDocument /
linkDocumentContent / ensureLinkFolderId). Both v4 and the Rust port run the same
write sequence against the same mount-index fixture and the resulting four tables
structural-diffed in the minted-values REMAP form (ids minted internally, timestamps
minted internally → first-seen id-token remap across all four tables + timestamp
placeholders). The store row (doc_mount_points) is seeded with a pinned id so every
write's mountPointId is identical on both sides (NOT remapped).
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one every other
tier-2 fixture uses; it decrypts nothing real.
Scenarios: (1) a top-level JSON file → permissive policy; (2) a top-level markdown
file with no frontmatter → permissive; (3) a markdown file in a subfolder → exercises
ensureLinkFolderId creating the Knowledge folder; (4) a second path with content
byte-identical to (2) → dedup-by-sha (reuses one file + one document row, new link);
(5) rewriting path (2) with new content → link upsert-in-place + a new file/document
row (the old ones survive, still referenced by (4)); (6) a markdown file whose
frontmatter sets character_read:false → the policy cascade forces all three allow*
flags to 0, verified against v4's real yaml-based policyFromContent.

ops.content:
# Crew

The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

---
character_read: false

For the operator's eyes only.
